raw_input:
[4.0/5] My favorite roller shape I have a lot of facial rollers. The Y shape is my favorite. The gentle suction massage is very relaxing. This one has a nice weight and feels well made. It rolls smoothly and silently. It came in a velvet pouch in a sealed bubble bag. It's competitively priced right now at $13.99 plus an extra 40% off. [5.0/5] Great Skincare Tool This is definitely good quality! The roller itself has a cooling effect when applied on the skin, even without any skin care product. Instructions are easy to read/follow. [5.0/5] Face massager This is a relaxing tool. It feels nice going across the cheeks. It glides easily. It is also pretty. [5.0/5] Wow, I am impressed! I have a bigger version of this tool for the body that is also silver/chrome but it is not well made and looks shoddy compared to this because it is probably not metal and made of plastic. I didn't have any expectations for this but was so pleasantly surprised by how nice it is. It looks very fancy and feels really good in my hand and on my face. It's easy to roll around on my face and jawline without having to put face oil. I have Gua Sha tool with a roller at the end that costs 3x this and they're both in the same caliber imo. Get this, you will love it! And at this low price of $14, get one for your friend! [4.0/5] Feels good. Heavy duty construction. Not sure of any health claims. I don't know about the validity of any of the health claims. The Massager is solidly constructed and does feel good when run on my face and arms. You need to be careful and let the massages roll without putting excessive downward pressure. If you push too hard, it can leave your face feeling uncomfortable and even in some pain. Lite pressure and my face and arms feel great. I will use this to give a massage on my face and arms. [5.0/5] Great This is a beautiful face roller. It feels good in the hands and is definitely a high quality beauty tool. [4.0/5] Nice, but what kind of metal? The quality is clearly visible in the construction and the extremely smooth rolling mechanism. There’s a nice weighty feel to this as well that adds a little something extra to the whole massage or lymphatic draining experience. Honestly I’m really happy with this and have been enjoying using it. My only complaint is the lack of transparency regarding the material, which is only described as “Metal” both online and in the provided pamphlet. For anyone who has metal sensitivities or a nickel allergy - I’d suggest being cautious about using this and maybe starting with a patch test on a small portion of your body before diving into using it on your face. [4.0/5] Well engineered face massager [[VIDEOID:c94a2c2720b868dbd87475026003c182]] This is a review of " YUNRUO Face Roller Face Massager, Metal Facial Roller, Face Lift Beauty Roller for Neck Eye Body Skin Care”
[As a vine reviewer, I am pledging not to be compensated or influenced by the vendor; I state my opinions based on my personal experience without any bias or pressure]
[I am not a doctor and therefore the review below should be viewed against this disclaimer]

In the interest of full disclosure, I should start by stating that I had never tried any face roller until a few weeks back . So clearly it was with a bit of skepticism that I tried this product, primarily because the looks appealed to me .

This face massager is made with a steel Y- shaped stem and two little oval plastic balls with geometrical patterns inside. The idea is to run this massager on your face, a bit like a razor, and the moving movement is expected to shape your face. For someone who is often teased for a double chin I think this product may work well. The most interesting thing about this product is how well made it really is. The two balls do I have a grabbing kind of effect and if you run it along your jaw lines, it does kill the impression off compressing the facial muscle. Like all such products, the efficacy cannot be ascertained until you use it for some time. There is even a well-illustrated pamphlet that comes with a massager in a nice stain carrying pouch.
But just going by this ergonomically designed massager, I am willing to give it four stars for now. A couple of words of caution: the steel stem is probably rust proof, but I would avoid moisture. Similarly, the balls are made of plastic, although they seem reasonably study, I would still urge a bit of care. But on the whole, this has been a very fun and satisfying experience so far using this product. [5.0/5] relaxing and smooth very relaxing face massager , the metal roller is smooth and provide nice massage to face skin, also very nice on the neck, it come is small bag for carrying [5.0/5] works well This product is well made and designed, made from the solid steel, and the surface is well polished, the handle is comfortable to hold. The rollers are small and have no issue to roll over my face and neck, and around my hands and arms as well. I can use it while I am doing my job. Comparing with other rollers, this rollers have a central toward pushing/pulling force depending the rolling direction, which provides an extra comfort when moving it over the skin. It works well on the small muscles. I like the feeling while the rollers move over my skins, and my muscles had a nice relaxation after the operation. Definitely, like it. [5.0/5] works well This product is well made and designed, made from the solid steel, and the surface is well polished, the handle is comfortable to hold. The rollers are small and have no issue to roll over my face and neck, and around my hands and arms as well. I can use it while I am doing my job. Comparing with other rollers, this rollers have a central toward pushing/pulling force depending the rolling direction, which provides an extra comfort when moving it over the skin. It works well on the small muscles. I like the feeling while the rollers move over my skins, and my muscles had a nice relaxation after the operation. Definitely, like it. [5.0/5] works well This product is well made and designed, made from the solid steel, and the surface is well polished, the handle is comfortable to hold. The rollers are small and have no issue to roll over my face and neck, and around my hands and arms as well. I can use it while I am doing my job. Comparing with other rollers, this rollers have a central toward pushing/pulling force depending the rolling direction, which provides an extra comfort when moving it over the skin. It works well on the small muscles. I like the feeling while the rollers move over my skins, and my muscles had a nice relaxation after the operation. Definitely, like it. [5.0/5] Feels Sooooo Good! I think I am in love with this face massager. It feels so good and slightly pulls the skin in between the two rolling balls, but in a good way. The rolling balls look like little disco balls and the entire roller is made of sturdy materials.

I have been wanting a facial roller because I heard that it's good to help drain your lymphatic ducts and stimulate blood flow in your face. But the added sensation of your face being gently sucked on (I know, that doesn't sound good but trust me!) is AMAZING.

For the price, this thing is a steal. [4.0/5] Good 3D rollers! These metal rollers have a 3-D design and this give a good kneading affect. These are of course, waterproof as they are made of good quality metal. A very easy and effective option to feel my face firmer. [5.0/5] Perfect. I like how it feels on my face. It well give you the best message for your face while sitting in your home watching TV. Easy to use and easy to clean . If you want it a little colder, just toss it in the freezer for couple minutes. Love it. [5.0/5] My favorite facial tool I love how relaxing this is for your face. Easy to use around the jaw line & eye area. The quality is great for the price. [5.0/5] Great face massager You can use this face massager with the lotion or facial serum of your choice. It does a great job distributing the serum on your face and massaging it in, the rollers are cool and soothing. Even my boyfriend likes to use it! [2.0/5] Title and description are a little misleading... The wife and the MIL are huge fans of these face rollers, so we've tried just about every version and model out there. Although this does have a nice heavy, metal handle, the balls are PLASTIC. Stating in the title that it's a "Metal Facial Roller" is misleading because there are models where everything is indeed metal. In the description, it also says "our facial roller tool is High quality metal material". Ummmm... that's JUST THE HANDLE. The models we've tried with plastic balls had the fake metal paint water off, and some of them even crack after a while, so this is important in determining quality of the tool and the price that it should command. Most of the ones with plastic are priced around $8-10, while the all metal ones are $24-30. That being said, the price of this one might seem like a good deal if you think it's an all metal model - but it is actually overpriced for the plastic-ball-model. [4.0/5] Nice weight to it! I’d ordered a cheaper roller in the past and was disappointed by how light and flimsy it was, so I was pleased with the weight of this model. It feels substantial and well-made. There’s a bit of a learning curve to using it that has to do with the angle at which you hold the handle as you roll in each direction. The included instructions were a bit sparse but gave me an idea, at least. I haven’t seen significant changes in my skin from using this device, but it feels good, at least!

response:
Y-shaped face roller. Two small oval rolling balls with geometric patterns create gentle suction and kneading effect on skin. Steel handle, smooth and silent rolling mechanism. Comes with velvet pouch. Priced around $14.

Most reviewers love feel on face, jaw, neck, cheeks. Relaxing, glides easily, good for distributing serums and oils. Feels cool on skin. Can toss in freezer for extra cooling effect. Sturdy, weighty feel adds to massage experience. Some use on arms and body too.

Caution on pressure - too hard causes discomfort. Use light touch. One reviewer notes handle likely steel but balls are plastic, not fully metal as advertised. Calls this misleading since true all-metal rollers cost more. Another reviewer with metal sensitivities or nickel allergy suggests patch test before using on face.

Material transparency issue noted by multiple buyers. Pamphlet included but instructions sparse. No proven medical claims though some buyers report lymphatic drainage and blood flow benefits.

Overall most buyers happy with purchase at price point. Many say good value compared to similar tools costing 3x more. Several bought as gifts for friends and family.
